evidence_quotes.quote: Practices in the EULA of some games or products of some particular companies, outright can be considered unreasonable, they claim that they have the right to make the product i pay for unusable whenever they want to.

Another thing is if they for some reason cannot do any of these for whatever reason, at least i should be able to get a refund for a product or service that i wasn't able to use for the promised amount of time.

paragraphs.text: As a consumer, i don't appreciate when companies make my game or any other product for that matter unusuable, of course they do have the right to not provide support forever, however i as a consumer at the very least should be notified during my purchase that when that service is going to end or even after the discontinuation of the service the product even if it is in its lesser form should be usable or for games should be playable. Afterall, i am paying for a product and i should have right to keep the product, if this is defined as a service however, then i should know when it is going to reach to its end of life. Practices in the EULA of some games or products of some particular companies, outright can be considered unreasonable, they claim that they have the right to make the product i pay for unusable whenever they want to. Another thing is if they for some reason cannot do any of these for whatever reason, at least i should be able to get a refund for a product or service that i wasn't able to use for the promised amount of time. In short, companies should not be able to destroy a product or service whenever they please to, this hurts me as a customer both economically and emotionally, afterall, im not just paying for the product, in case of games im also putting my time and effort into it, this might seem small at first but considering games are now a part of a very big culture, they could be considered a cultural heritage and loss of them is certainly a cultural loss for society, we can think of them as board games or sports of ancient times that's been lost to history, wouldn't it be a massive cultural lost to lose an entire board game from couple centuries ago to the dusty pages of the past? considering today is the future's past, we should be allowed to keep such product or services, this would overall benefit society both culture-wise and consumer wise. Thank you for giving us a voice.
